messages.content:
I'd like to know more about the models in the Playground and, if possible, the models that we'll be evaluated on in April.

How are they quantized? How big are context windows? Can responses time out?

Hi, I'm getting an error when trying to test on Gemini: 'Need 24.9 credits but only have 8.0' Is this expected? The credit cost seems much higher than other models. Is there a way to test Gemini within the standard credit allocation? Need 8.4 credits but only have 8.0. Grok

@_**Garbid|157** [said](https://zulip.sair.foundation/#narrow/channel/9-general/topic/Models/near/1194):
```quote
Hi, I'm getting an error when trying to test on Gemini: 'Need 24.9 credits but only have 8.0' Is this expected? The credit cost seems much higher than other models. Is there a way to test Gemini within the standard credit allocation? Need 8.4 credits but only have 8.0. Grok
```



You probably are selecting too many multiple equations at once? run batches of two equations at a time for Gemini, as the hold token vs cost token is very high for Gemini

Quick question: when re-running the same cheatsheet on the same problems, I notice results vary by 1-2 problems each time. Is this expected due to model temperature settings? And will the final evaluation use temperature=0 or default temperature? This affects how we should interpret our test results.

@_**Garbid|157** [писал/а](https://zulip.sair.foundation/#narrow/channel/9-general/topic/Models/near/1204):
```quote
Quick question: when re-running the same cheatsheet on the same problems, I notice results vary by 1-2 problems each time. Is this expected due to model temperature settings? And will the final evaluation use temperature=0 or default temperature? This affects how we should interpret our test results.
```

Sorry, found the answer in the rules - the evaluation model will be announced by April 10. Thanks!
This topic was moved here from #**general>Models** by @_**Terence Tao|28**.
